title: Question: Get a signal from DPI on every eval() called.
body:
--- Author Name: **HyungKi Jeong** Original Redmine Message: 2368 from https://www.veripool.org --- I just want to make CLK signal inside my verilog code.
So I coded a example as like below.
But not working as I think.
How can I execute my DPI function on every single 'eval()' call?
Or can I share the verilog CLK signal's actual pointer value to DPI function. ``` import "DPI-C" function void GetCurrentClock(output bit clk); reg CLK; always@(*) begin GetCurrentClock(CLK); end ```
